title: Tong On Viet Lai Cau Dong Nghia

knowledge.html:
Tong On Viet Lai Cau Dong Nghia

Viet lai cau sao cho nghia khong doi, su dung tu/cum tu cho san. Bao gom ca 2 dang: viet lai cau tu dau va viet lai cau voi tu cho san trong ngoac.

exercises.original: "Don't forget to lock the door," the mother said to the boy.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: The mother reminded the boy to lock the door.
acceptedAnswers: The mother reminded the boy to lock the door.
The mother reminded the boy to lock the door
The mother reminded the boy not to forget to lock the door.
The mother reminded the boy not to forget to lock the door
exercises.explanation: Don't forget = reminded + object + to V.

exercises.original: The mother made the boy tidy up the room.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: The boy was made to tidy up the room by the mother.
acceptedAnswers: The boy was made to tidy up the room by the mother.
The boy was made to tidy up the room.
The boy was made to tidy up the room by his mother.
The boy was made to tidy up the room by his mother
The boy was made to tidy up the room by the mother
The boy was made to tidy up the room
exercises.explanation: Passive of 'make': S + was made + to V.

exercises.original: After he had graduated from university, he went abroad to work.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Having graduated from university, he went abroad to work.
acceptedAnswers: Having graduated from university, he went abroad to work.
Having graduated from university, he went abroad to work
exercises.explanation: After + had V3 = Having + V3 (perfect participle).

exercises.original: Housewives do not have to spend a lot of time doing housework anymore.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: No longer do housewives have to spend a lot of time doing housework.
acceptedAnswers: No longer do housewives have to spend a lot of time doing housework.
No longer do housewives have to spend a lot of time doing housework
exercises.explanation: Not...anymore = No longer + inversion (auxiliary + S + V).

exercises.original: It is said that Galileo invented the telescope.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Galileo is said to have invented the telescope.
acceptedAnswers: Galileo is said to have invented the telescope.
Galileo is said to have invented the telescope
exercises.explanation: It is said that + S + V2/ed = S + is said + to have + V3.

exercises.original: The last time I met Mary was three years ago.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: I haven't met Mary for three years.
acceptedAnswers: I haven't met Mary for three years.
I haven't met Mary for three years
I have not met Mary for three years.
I have not met Mary for three years
exercises.explanation: The last time + V2/ed + was + time + ago = haven't + V3 + for + time.

exercises.original: What does your mother do?
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: What is your mother's job?
acceptedAnswers: What is your mother's job?
What is your mother's occupation?
What is your mother's job
exercises.explanation: What does someone do = What is someone's job.

exercises.original: I am not a rich businessman and I can't afford to buy an expensive car.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: If I were a rich businessman, I could afford to buy an expensive car.
acceptedAnswers: If I were a rich businessman, I could afford to buy an expensive car.
If I were a rich businessman, I could afford to buy an expensive car
If I was a rich businessman, I could afford to buy an expensive car.
If I was a rich businessman, I could afford to buy an expensive car
exercises.explanation: Second conditional for unreal present situations.

exercises.original: I spent an hour revising what he had already learnt for the test.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: It took me an hour to revise what he had already learnt for the test.
acceptedAnswers: It took me an hour to revise what he had already learnt for the test.
It took me an hour to revise what he had already learnt for the test
exercises.explanation: S + spent + time + V-ing = It took + S + time + to V.

exercises.original: My father would rather read newspaper than listen to the radio.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: My father prefers reading newspaper to listening to the radio.
acceptedAnswers: My father prefers reading newspaper to listening to the radio.
My father prefers reading newspapers to listening to the radio.
My father prefers reading newspaper to listening to the radio
My father prefers reading the newspaper to listening to the radio.
exercises.explanation: Would rather + V + than + V = prefers + V-ing + to + V-ing.

exercises.original: The old man is working in this company. I love his daughter.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (whose)
exercises.answer: The old man whose daughter I love is working in this company.
acceptedAnswers: The old man whose daughter I love is working in this company.
The old man, whose daughter I love, is working in this company.
exercises.explanation: Relative pronoun 'whose' for possession.

exercises.original: Nam defeated the former champion in three sets. Then he won their inter-school table tennis championship.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (Having)
exercises.answer: Having defeated the former champion in three sets, Nam won their inter-school table tennis championship.
acceptedAnswers: Having defeated the former champion in three sets, Nam won their inter-school table tennis championship.
Having defeated the former champion in three sets, Nam won their inter-school table tennis championship
exercises.explanation: Perfect participle: Having + V3, S + V2/ed.

exercises.original: Although she was disabled, she was quite confident the first time she practiced this sport.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (Despite)
exercises.answer: Despite being disabled, she was quite confident the first time she practiced this sport.
acceptedAnswers: Despite being disabled, she was quite confident the first time she practiced this sport.
Despite her disability, she was quite confident the first time she practiced this sport.
exercises.explanation: Although + clause = Despite + V-ing/noun.

exercises.original: Mary doesn't like music. Her brother doesn't either.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (Neither)
exercises.answer: Neither Mary nor her brother likes music.
acceptedAnswers: Neither Mary nor her brother likes music.
Neither Mary nor her brother like music.
exercises.explanation: Neither...nor connects two negative ideas.

exercises.original: The students will be awarded the present. The students' reports are very valuable.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (whose)
exercises.answer: The students whose reports are very valuable will be awarded the present.
acceptedAnswers: The students whose reports are very valuable will be awarded the present.
The students whose reports are very valuable will be awarded the present
exercises.explanation: Relative pronoun 'whose' for possession.

exercises.original: Jane didn't change her working style. She was sacked.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (If)
exercises.answer: If Jane had changed her working style, she wouldn't have been sacked.
acceptedAnswers: If Jane had changed her working style, she wouldn't have been sacked.
If Jane had changed her working style, she would not have been sacked.
If Jane had changed her working style, she wouldn't have been sacked
If Jane had changed her working style, she would not have been sacked
exercises.explanation: Third conditional for unreal past situations.

exercises.original: Hai had all the necessary qualifications for the job. He wasn't offered the job.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (Despite)
exercises.answer: Despite having all the necessary qualifications for the job, Hai wasn't offered the job.
acceptedAnswers: Despite having all the necessary qualifications for the job, Hai wasn't offered the job.
Despite having all the necessary qualifications, Hai wasn't offered the job.
Despite having all the necessary qualifications for the job, Hai was not offered the job.
exercises.explanation: Although + clause = Despite + V-ing.

exercises.original: She closed the door. She wanted to focus on the lesson.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (to)
exercises.answer: She closed the door to focus on the lesson.
acceptedAnswers: She closed the door to focus on the lesson.
She closed the door in order to focus on the lesson.
exercises.explanation: Purpose: to + V / in order to + V.

exercises.original: He was betrayed by his partner. He suffered huge losses.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (Betrayed)
exercises.answer: Betrayed by his partner, he suffered huge losses.
acceptedAnswers: Betrayed by his partner, he suffered huge losses.
Betrayed by his partner, he suffered huge losses
exercises.explanation: Past participle clause: V3 + by..., S + V.

exercises.original: She doesn't love him. He is sad.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (If)
exercises.answer: If she loved him, he wouldn't be sad.
acceptedAnswers: If she loved him, he wouldn't be sad.
If she loved him, he would not be sad.
If she loved him, he wouldn't be sad
If she loved him, he would not be sad
exercises.explanation: Second conditional for unreal present situations.
